source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In Fig. 5.10 a point charge $e$ moves with constant velocity $v$ in the $z$ direction so that at time $t$ it is at the point $Q$ with coordinates $x=0$, $y=0, z=v t$. Find at the time $t$ and at the point $P$ with coordinates $x=b, y=0, z=0$ the scalar potential $\phi$.
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL

image_dependency: 题干提到“Fig. 5.10”，且样本字段 `requires_image=true`、`text_dominant=false`，说明上游将其登记为依赖图像的物理题。
但题干已明确给出运动方向、时刻位置 Q 的坐标以及求值点 P 的坐标，核心几何关系在文字中基本可重建，因此图像不是唯一信息来源。
图像更像是空间布置的辅助示意，而非必须读取数值或视觉细节的图表。
multi_step: 需要结合带电粒子匀速运动、观察点坐标、时间变量 `t` 与速度参数 `v`/`beta`，不是单步代入即可直接得到。
标准求解通常涉及电磁学中的相对论势函数或等价变换，并将空间距离关系化为最终封闭表达式，存在明显的多步推导。
答案是含 `b`、`v`、`t`、`beta` 的解析式，表明任务重在推导而非简单读图或单次计算。
verifiability: 答案类型为 `short_text`，且给出了明确的单一公式，便于后续按符号形式核验。
题干中的变量定义清楚，答案可通过物理公式推导或符号等价检查进行验证。
`registry_passed=true`，题目文本、答案和图像资产均已登记，基础核验条件较好。

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: In Fig. 5.10 a point charge $e$ moves with constant velocity $v$ in the $z$ direction so that at time $t$ it is at the point $Q$ with coordinates $x=0$, $y=0, z=v t$. Find at the time $t$ and at the point $P$ with coordinates $x=b, y=0, z=0$ the scalar potential $\phi$.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object string representation rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain:
Solution:
(a) The Lienard-Wiechert potentials at $P$ due to the charge are given by
$$
\phi= rac{e}{4 \pi \varepsilon_{0}\left[r- rac{\mathbf{v}}{c} \cdot \mathbf{r}ight]}, \quad \mathbf{A}= rac{e \mathbf{V}}{4 \pi \varepsilon_{0} c^{2}\left[r- rac{\mathbf{V}}{c} \cdot \mathbf{r}ight]}
$$
where $\mathbf{r}$ is the radius vector from the retarded position of the charge to the field point $P$, i.e.,
$$
\mathbf{r}=b \mathbf{e}_{x}-v\left(t- rac{r}{c}ight) \mathbf{e}_{z}=b \mathbf{e}_{x}-v t^{\prime} \mathbf{e}_{z}
$$
with
$$
t^{\prime}=t- rac{r}{c}.
$$
Thus
$$
r^{2}=\mathbf{r} \cdot \mathbf{r}=b^{2}+v^{2}\left(t- rac{r}{c}ight)^{2}=b^{2}+v^{2}\left(t^{2}- rac{2 r t}{c}+ rac{r^{2}}{c^{2}}ight),
$$
or
$$
\left(1- rac{v^{2}}{c^{2}}ight) r^{2}+2  rac{v^{2} t}{c} r-b^{2}-v^{2} t^{2}=0.
$$
This is the retardation condition, with the solutions
$$
r= rac{- eta v t \pm \sqrt{\left(1- eta^{2}ight) b^{2}+v^{2} t^{2}}}{1- eta^{2}}
$$
where $ eta= rac{v}{c}$.
However the upper sign is to be taken since $r \geq 0$. As $v=v e_{z}$,
$$
 egin{aligned}
r- rac{\mathbf{v} \cdot \mathbf{r}}{c} & =r+ rac{v^{2} t^{\prime}}{c}=r+v  eta\left(t- rac{r}{c}ight)=\left(1- eta^{2}ight) r+v  eta t \
& =\sqrt{\left(1- eta^{2}ight) b^{2}+v^{2} t^{2}}
\end{aligned}
$$
The scalar potential $\phi$ is then
$$
\phi= rac{e}{4 \pi \varepsilon_{0} \sqrt{\left(1- eta^{2}ight) b^{2}+v^{2} t^{2}}}.
$$
